summary: Create a new applicant or candidate for new hires
description: This operation creates a new job applicant and returns a new applicantId. You can review applicants and their data on the Job Candidates form in PrismHR; to look up information for the applicant you created, enter the applicantId value in the Candidate Number field. This operation enables import of applicants/new hire candidates into PrismHR from an external source, such as an applicant tracking system. Since these external systems may vary substantially, this operation is designed to be as flexible as possible, with minimal restrictions and validations on the input fields.

summary: Get client benefit work flow grid
description: Use this operation to retrieve the fields in the client benefit workflow grid. Depending on the input, this method can return data for workflows with specific start dates at the client level and/or the system level. Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. This operation is used to retrieve client work flow grid data from PrismHR for use by external programs To use this API, provide a client ID(optional) and desired oe start/end date range(optional). This will initialize the data retrieval and will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the compiled JSON data object may be retrieved. The JSON object obtained at the URL in dataObject will have the following structure: { "data": [ { "clientWorkFlowGrid": [ { "clientId": "string", "workflowEffDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "betWFId": "string", "beWFDesc": "string", "windowLength": "string" "oeWFId": "string" "oeWFDesc": "string" "anytimeWFId": "string" "anytimeWFDesc": "string" "oeStartDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "oeEndDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "minOverlapDays": "string" "endEffDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "enableNotifications": "string" } ], "systemWorkFlowGrid": [ { "workflowEffDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "betWFId": "string", "beWFDesc": "string", "windowLength": "string" "oeWFId": "string" "oeWFDesc": "string" "anytimeWFId": "string" "anytimeWFDesc": "string" "oeStartDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "oeEndDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "minOverlapDays": "string" "endEffDate": "YYYY-MM-DD" } ] } ] }

description: The benefits/import401KData method uploads 401K import data, performs validations against the data, and then writes the new data to PrismHR. The response identifies those lines in the import data that did not pass validation, with error messages for each line. Rows that do pass validation are imported even if some rows contain errors. This method can be called repeatedly, and overwrites the previous upload. This allows the client application to review the response, correct problems, and then upload the method again without duplicating records; note employees not included in subsequent uploads are not deleted. The provider parameter determines which import utility is used. Currently, the only supported provider is Slavic 401K, so this operation defaults to that import utility if no provider is specified. The allowedEmpStatusClasses is optional and allows the user to specify which employee status classes (active, terminated, and leave) will pass validation. For example, providing "AL" for allowedEmpStatusClasses will allow the import on employees with status classes of A (active) and L (leave). Omitting this parameter will default allowedEmpStatusClasses to A. The 401K plan ID may be provided via the planId input parameter (if the entire import is for the same 401K plan) or in the comma separated import data. If no planId is provided in the input, then an error will be returned for each import line that does not contain a valid planId. if a planId is provided in the input, then this is the plan under which all import lines will be imported ignoring any plan ID data provided in the import data.ProviderRequired Import DataExample Import DataSlavic1. The client ID 2. The employee's social security number 3. Plan effective date (MM/DD/YY) 4. Plan deferral type (D - Pre-Tax, R - After Tax, N - Non-Elective) 5. Plan contribution type (1 - Flat Amount, 2 - Percent) 6. Flat amount (if contribution type = 1) 7. Percent (if contribution type = 2) 8. (Optional) Line item 401K plan ID 9. (Optional) Match Starts On Date (MM/DD/YY) 10.(Optional) Take Flat On Supplemental (valid values for "true": true, T, Y , 1. Valid values for "false": any other value, or empty.) 11. Unused 12. Catch-up override deferral type (D - Pre-Tax, R - After Tax) If used, you must also specify the contribution type and set the flat and percentage amounts. 13. Catch-up override contribution type (1 - Flat Amount, 2 - Percent). 14. Catch-up override flat amount (if catchup contribution type = 1). 15. Catch-up override percent (if catchup contribution type = 2).000123,012345678,02/01/20,D,1,25,,401KPLANA 000123,123456789,02/01/20,D,2,,3,401KPLANB 123456,234567890,02/01/20,R,1,25,,401KPLANB 123456,345678901,02/01/20,R,2,,3,401KPLANA 123456,456789012,02/01/20,N,,,,401KPLANA,02/01/20,Y 123456,456789012,02/01/20,N,,,,401KPLANA,02/01/20,Y,,D,1,10,, 123456,456789012,02/01/20,N,,,,401KPLANA,02/01/20,Y,,R,2,,10

fields.description: loan payment cycle - (P)er pay period or (M)onthly

fields.name: importData

fields.name: allowedEmpStatusClasses
fields.description: employee status classes to allow in the import. Options are: (A)ctive, (T)erminated, (L)eave, and (P)rismHR Settings. If P, the API allows any status classes listed in the eligibility settings of the client- or system-level retirement plan. If no status classes are listed in the retirement plan setup, then all classes are included in the import. P overrides any other value passed in this field. If this field is empty, the default value is (A)ctive.

description: Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. This operation is used to generate and download a retirement census report. To use this API, provide a report format (Census, Participants, or Eligibility) and any other desired filter criteria. This will initialize the data retrieval and will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the compiled CSV formatted report may be retrieved. Note 1: Access to this API's report formats can be controlled using option-level access control. See the Option-level Access Control article in the API documentation for details. Note 2: By default, this operation masks certain personally identifiable information (PII) in its response, such as employee social security number and date of birth. Please refer to the API documentation article Unmasking PII to learn how to unmask this data. Note 3: The web service user client restrictions for this operation are handled differently from other PrismHR Services API operations. If the web service user has any client restrictions the user is required to provide a clientId up to 10 separated by commas.

To enter a contact address, you must provide at least a ZIP code and a two-character state code. You can also provide a city name, but the endpoint will return an error if this does not match the internal city value for the GeoCode. To obtain a list of valid cities, states, and Geolocation codes for a particular ZIP, call ClientMasterService.getGeolocations. If the provided ZIP and state correspond to multiple unique Geolocation codes, this endpoint returns an error stating that the Geolocation data was ambiguous. To resolve this error, obtain the correct GeoCode from getGeolocations and pass that value in the geolocationCode request attribute.

summary: Get code tables for the specified client
description: This method returns one or more code tables as arrays (lists) for the specified client. Within the Options string parameter, specify one or more of the option names in the table below. Note that the names are not case sensitive. Note that with shift codes, many of the data elements will be empty depending on whether the client is configured to use mileage mode or shift mode as configured in the Client Master/Detail parameters. This operation also returns paginated results for the option Project. Use the count and startpage query parameters to navigate through the list of projects. count specifies the number of projects to return per page, and startpage indicates the starting position in the project list. The operation returns these parameters in the response object as well, along with the total number of projects. We will be implementing pagination for other client code types in the future. Note: This endpoint returns client-level codes by default. You can return system-level codes for certain options by prefixing the option name with “SYSTEM.” For example, SYSTEM.Status returns system-level status codes. description: This operation returns a list of all retirement benefit plans set up under the Benefits tab on the Client Details form in PrismHR for all or up to 10 selected clients. This operation also returns paginated results. Use the count and startpage query parameters to navigate through the list of retirement benefit plans. count specifies the number of clients to return per page, and startpage indicates the starting position in the client list. The operation returns these parameters in the response object as well, along with the total number of clients. If the number of clients returned from the query is greater than 5000, you will be required to enter a count value of 5000 or under and a startpage. Note: The web service user client restrictions for this operation are handled differently from other PrismHR Services API operations. If the web service user has any client restrictions the user is required to provide a clientId up to 10 separated by commas. If there are no client restrictions then clientId can be passed as ALL to return all active clients or client cancelling (status C or A). If you have access to all clients, pass ALL to get a list of all retirement benefit plans for all active clients or client cancelling (status C or A) or provide one or more clientIds (up to 10) separated by commas. If some of the clients are passed for which you do not have access, then the retirement benefit plans for those restricted clients will not be returned. If you do not have access to all the clients passed, then you will receive an HTTP Response Code 403 with an infoMessage stating Company “clientId” is not allowed and data has not been returned for the company. If an invalid clientId is passed, then you will receive an errorMessage stating Company Id ‘clientId’ not found.

description: This operation returns information about client-level and (if allowed) employer-level state unemployment tax rates (SUTA rates). You can enter "ALLCLIENTS" or "ALLEMPLOYERS" to return SUTA rates for all entities. Enter an effectiveDate to return SUTA rates effective on that date. Otherwise, provide a fromDate, which returns SUTA rates that are effective on or after that date. Note: This endpoint has option-level security applied to the employerId field. To use this field, in the Allowed Methods grid for the web service user, enter ClientMasterService.getSutaRates#EMPLOYER. For more details, see the Option-level Access Control article on the API documentation website. This operation also returns paginated results when the total of client and employer SUTA records exceeds 500. Use the count and startpage query parameters to navigate through the list of SUTA rates. count specifies the number of SUTA rates to return per page, and startpage indicates the starting position in the SUTA rates list. The operation returns these parameters in the response object as well, along with the total number of SUTA rates. Certain states have additional state-specific taxes. These rates are set in the stateMiscRate fields (1, 2, and 3). State-specific employee-paid tax rates are set in the stateMiscEmpRate2 and stateMiscEmpRate3 fields. See the online PrismHR documentation for up-to-date details about these fields.

description: Use this endpoint to update the information for an existing contact at a specific client managed in PrismHR. A checksum is required to avoid a conflict with other transactions that might be in flight. To obtain the checksum, call the ClientMasterService.getAllPrismClientContacts or ClientMasterService.getPrismClientContact endpoints. This endpoint requires that all data retrieved from the corresponding get operation be supplied in the request object along with the provided checksum. Any omitted data will be deleted from the database. The checksum is used to ensure that the data retrieved from the database is up-to-date when writing the updates back to the record. Note: This endpoint validates contact addresses against internal Geolocation codes. To enter a contact address, you must provide at least a ZIP code and a two-character state code. You can also provide a city name, but the endpoint will return an error if this does not match the internal city value for the GeoCode. To obtain a list of valid cities, states, and Geolocation codes for a particular ZIP, call ClientMasterService.getGeolocations. If the provided ZIP and state correspond to multiple unique Geolocation codes, this endpoint returns an error stating that the Geolocation data was ambiguous. To resolve this error, obtain the correct GeoCode from getGeolocations and pass that value in the geolocationCode request attribute.

description: This service manages what can most easily be described as the "rules" for employee payroll deductions, rather than precise values (which must be determined by looking at payroll vouchers after payroll is completed). There are three groups of deductions. Voluntary deductions, such as uniforms or movie tickets, are fixed amounts or percentages. These are added immediately to the deductions file when they are added to the system. The first group of non-voluntary deductions, such as garnishments and loans, are immediately added by the respective modules in PrismHR. The final group of deductions are those for enrolled benefit plans. The process for computing these is more complex, and it is not added to deductions immediately when the enrollment changes or benefits plan is altered. Instead, the deductions are updated at the time the organization processes payroll.

description: Use this operation to rehire an employee. description: The EmployeeService.setI9Data method updates an employee's form I-9 information. Note that a checksum attribute is required and is used to avoid a conflict with other transactions that may be in flight. To obtain the checksum, call the EmployeeService.getI9Data method. I9 document codes and document issuing authority codes may be found by calling ClientMasterService.getClientCodes with the I9Docs option. For certain I9 documents that do not have any issuing authorities specified, either provide "N/A" as the code or omit the phrIssuingAuthorityCode parameter; in either case the API will save "N/A" to the database as the issuing authority code. The checksum is used to ensure that the data retrieved from the database is up-to-date when writing the updates back to the record.

description: This operation updates direct deposit account information for checking and savings accounts as well as pay cards. This will not modify other ACH types such as FSA (flexible spending accounts). Changes to those other direct deposit settings must be made in the PrismHR product. Note that pay card accounts must be specially configured (in either product) as a pay card in Bank Routing and then set up as a checking account. The updateDirectDeposit operation replaces the entire record, specifically checking and saving account information, therefore you must ensure that you include any existing accounts that are not changing as well as any new accounts. Pay cards cannot be deleted using updateDirectDeposit, only marked inactive. You will not be able to change a direct deposit account to active if there are existing active line items for the account. Please contact your administrator if you want to make it active. Also, a checksum attribute is required, to avoid a conflict with other transactions that might be in flight. To obtain the existing direct deposit record (achVoucher) and the directDepositChecksum, call the EmployeeService.getEmployee operation with the DirectDeposit option.

description: Use this operation to update individual employee fields without the requirement of providing a checksum. The API will ignore any fields excluded from the input, as well as any fields set to null. Sub-Objects like vehicleDetails, autoPolicy and localTax must include all fields and their values when updating any field within that object.The operation returns checksums for any employee record files that were updated. For example, it returns a compensationChecksum value for any changes to fields in the Employee Pay record.SecurityThis operation supports field-level security: for any web service user, you can grant or deny access to a subset of the supported employee fields.To set up these access settings, use the Allowed Methods grid on the Web Service Users form in PrismHR. Create a new row and add the name of this method. Then, to grant access to certain fields, append # or #GRANT, followed by a list of the accessible fields. Separate each field name with a pipe symbol. (|). The web service user will have access to only these fields.If you want to deny access to certain fields instead, append #DENY and a list of fields as described previously. The web service user will have access to any fields not specified in the list.Example grant access:EmployeeService.updateEmployeeFields#GRANT|firstName|lastNameEmployeeServ ice.updateEmployeeFields#firstName|lastNameExample deny access:EmployeeService.updateEmployeeFields#DENY|firstName|lastName Warning: Sub-Objects like vehicleDetails, autoPolicy, property and localTax must include all fields and their values when updating any field within that object. If all fields and arrays within the sub-object are not included then those values will be removed from the record.

description: This operation updates employee address information. Note that a checksum attribute is required, which is used to avoid a conflict with other transactions that might be in flight. To obtain the checksum, call the EmployeeService.getAddressInfo operation. Important note: If the zipCode, city, or county has changed for the home address and the API can determine a single matching geolocation code, it will update the geolocation code automatically. The endpoint will return an update message with a warning regarding any other address details that were changed as part of the geolocation match. If multiple geolocation options exist for the provided address, this endpoint returns an error and populates address suggestions that meet the provided criteria in the matchingValues array. In this case, update your request with the appropriate address details and call the endpoint again. If no geolocation matches exist, the endpoint will update the address information as provided in the request and will return a warning message similar to the one described above. This API requires that all data retrieved from the corresponding get operation be supplied in the request object along with the provided checksum. Any omitted data will be deleted from the database. The checksum is used to ensure that the data retrieved from the database is up-to-date when writing the updates back to the record.

description: Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. This operation is used to retrieve PEO client accounting data from PrismHR for use by external programs. To use this API, provide a client ID and either a pay date or post date range (you cannot use both filters). To retrieve all data on or before a specific date, enter an end date and leave the start date empty. To retrieve all data on or after a specific date, enter a start date and leave the end date empty. This API will initialize the data retrieval and will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the compiled JSON data object may be retrieved. The JSON object obtained at the URL in dataObject will have the following structure: { "clientId": "string", "startDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "endDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "data": [ { "payDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "batchId": "string", "onlineVoucherNo": "string", "annotation": "string", "period": "string", "consolidated": "string", "consolJrnlId": "string", "postFlag": "string", "batchJrnlIds": [ "string" ], "glDivision": "string", "dosFilename": "string", "postDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "postTime": "HH:MM:SS", "editDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "editUserId": "string", "periodEndDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "detailAnnotation": [ "string" ], "sumDebitAmt": "string", "sumCreditAmt": "string", "glInfo": [ { "glAccountNumber": "string", "debitAmount": "string", "creditAmount": "string", "hoursWorked": "string", "hoursUnitsPaid": "string" } ], "id": "string", "clientId": "string", "journalId": "string", "invoiceNo": "string" } ] }

description: Use this operation to download a JSON object summary of onboarding tasks for clients. You can also filter the results by clientList (comma separated client ids), task, or fromDate. Note 1: For information about properly using this operation to keep third-party application data synchronized, please see the getData Best Practices documentation. Note 2: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of 'DONE', retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. To use this API, provide a valid session token and any desired filter criteria. This will initialize the data retrieval and the API will respond with 'buildStatus': 'INIT'. Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either 'buildStatus': 'BUILD', 'buildStatus': 'ERROR', or 'buildStatus': 'DONE'. If buildStatus is DONE, then 'dataObject' will contain the URL where the compiled JSON data object may be retrieved. If there are client restrictions on the web service user, at least one clientId must be passed in the clientList field. Up to 10 client IDs may be passed at a time, separated by commas. If no clientList is entered and no client restrictions exist for the web service user, only data from active clients will be returned. The JSON object obtained at the URL in dataObject will have the following structure: { "clientId": "string", "task": "string", "prehireId": "string", "newHireName": "string", "performer": "string", "dueDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "submitDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "workflowDueDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "workflowCompleteDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "status": "string", "forms": [ { "formId": "string", "formName": "string", "formDueDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", "formCompleteDate": "YYYY-MM-DD", } ] }

summary: Import a batch of employee records
description: The PrismHR API employee import is a file-based utility similar to the PrismHR expanded employee Import. Please note that the "errorCode" parameter will only be non-zero if there is a problem with the entire employee import operation. If there are errors pertaining to an individual employee in the import, the "validFlag" parameter in the "importedHire" array for that employee will be set to false. It is possible that all employees in the import might have errors, in which case the "validFlag" for each employee would be false, but the "errorCode" would still be zero. Detailed error and/or warning messages pertaining to imported employees will appear in the "importError" array. This service has one important usage restriction: you can only have one import "batch" within each API session. If you need to perform concurrent new hire imports, you must use the LoginService to create a unique session for each concurrent thread. The reason for this is so that the API can isolate the batch to which the operations will apply. After you successfully import the new hire(s), you must commit the batch to add the employees to the database using NewHireService.commitEmployees. Please note that the required fields in the schema indicate only the minimum required fields. The service provider or client might have additional required fields set up in the system. Use the NewHireService.getNewHireRequiredFields method to retrieve a list of the fields required to hire new employees.

summary: Import a batch allowing a cross hire
description: The PrismHR API employee import is a file-based utility similar to the PrismHR expanded employee Import.The NewHireService.importEmployeesAllowingCrossHire operation is very similar to the importEmployees operation, with the additional option to hire an existing employee into an additional company. Your organization should consider restricting its use, instead of using it in place of the importEmployees operation. | Note: It is recommended that you restrict the use to service providers only. Typically, client companies would not import employees using this method because service providers must ensure the security and privacy of employees' data. A possible exception is when service providers decide to allow their franchise clients to enter cross-hires because those clients already have legitimate access to the employee information. | The key to the importEmployeesAllowingCrossHire operation is the crossCompanyEid parameter in the NewHireEmployee object, which you use to confirm to the system that you know the employee record already exists. This parameter is required; if you try to hire the existing employee into the company without this parameter, the system rejects it because it does not allow two employee records that use the same Social Security number. Please note that the required fields in the schema indicate only the minimum required fields. The service provider or client might have additional required fields set up in the system. Use the NewHireService.getNewHireRequiredFields method to retrieve a list of the fields required to hire new employees. After you successfully import the new hire(s), you must commit the batch to add the employees to the database using NewHireService.commitEmployees.

description: The PayrollService.calculateManualCheck operation calculates the taxes for the provided manual check, generates a voucher that the system includes in the client's next payroll batch, and returns the calculated check amounts. The payroll registers are updated when the payroll processor posts the batch. If calculationOnly is set to false, the check will not be saved to the payroll system. To delete a manual check prior to payroll batch processing, use the PayrollService.deleteManualCheck API operation; once manual checks have been initialized in a batch, they may not be deleted. Note about calculationOnly parameter: It is recommended that this operation be called with calculationOnly set to true and then, once the calculation results have been verified, called again with calculationOnly set to false in order to save the manual check. Note about payCodes parameter: If only the hoursUnitsPaid field is set, hoursWorked and payAmount will be calculated automatically. If these parameters are provided, the provided values will override the calculated values. Note about optionalTaxCalculationOverrides parameter: This array allows the calculated payroll taxes to be overridden with the provided static values. The check created will include only those payroll taxes provided in this array and will not include any calculated taxes.

summary: Calculate gross payment amount for a target net check amount
description: Please note that this API operation requires Prism and will not work in HRP. The PayrollService.calculateNetToGross method computes the gross amount that, after taxes and deductions, results in the amount of the check specified in targetNetPay. You can review the Batch Payment record for this employee and then write to batch by setting writeBatch to true. This updates the batch payments record. Note: the batch record associated with this payment should still be in Time Sheet Entry mode. If the batch has been initialized, it must be initialized again to retrieve this payment.

fields.name: payDate

fields.name: voucherType
fields.description: voucher type. Valid values are R (Regular), V (Vacation), S (Supplemental)

fields.name: payPeriod
fields.description: pay period that applies to the payment. The system calculates taxes based on the pay period. Valid values are W (Weekly), B (Biweekly), S (Semi-monthly), M (Monthly), Q (Quarterly), A (Annually)

fields.name: deductPeriod
fields.description: indicates the deduction period that the system should use for calculating standard deductions, benefits, and garnishments. Valid values are 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5. Select zero (0) if you do not want to include deductions in the calculation.

fields.description: the number of benefits deduction periods remaining; if not provided, the system will calculate the value based on the next scheduled regular batch

fields.name: payCode
fields.description: pay code that applies to the selected voucher type

fields.name: payrollNumber
fields.description: payroll number. Defaults to the next regularly scheduled payroll batch for the selected employee.

fields.name: writeBatch
fields.description: when set to false, this allows you to initially review the report details. To write this payment to the batch, run the method again with writeBatch set to true.

summary: Get bulk year to date values
description: Use this operation to download a JSON object summary of year-to-date values for different payroll variables, such as total deductions or total hours worked. You can also filter the results by client, employee, or a particular 'as of' date. If you submit a value in the voucherId input parameter, any value in the asOfDate parameter will be ignored. In addition, please note that this method only returns year-to-date data for up to one year, starting from January first of the current year or the year provided in asOfDate. Note 1: For information about properly using this operation to keep third-party application data synchronized, please see the getData Best Practices documentation. Note 2: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of 'DONE', retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. To use this API, provide a valid session token and any desired filter criteria. This will initialize the data retrieval and the API will respond with 'buildStatus': 'INIT'. Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either 'buildStatus': 'BUILD', 'buildStatus': 'ERROR', or 'buildStatus': 'DONE'. If buildStatus is DONE, then 'dataObject' will contain the URL where the compiled JSON data object may be retrieved. Please note: The web service user method and client restrictions for this operation are handled differently from other PrismHR Services API operations. Web service users need to be given explicit access to this API in the Allowed Methods list and they cannot have any client restrictions (similar to SystemService operations).

description: This operation returns a list of completed payroll batches for a given client, for the specified batch types and calendar year. description: Use this operation to finalize (post) a payroll batch. This initializes the asynchronous posting process; the API responds with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. It will return either "buildStatus": "WARN", "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, the batch has posted. If the system encounters payroll warnings, finalization is halted and warnings are returned in the warning array. When this occurs, review the warnings and fix any issues. If warnings exist and you want to finalize anyway, set bypassWarnings to true and call the endpoint again. If useApprovals is true, finalization is sent to the Approvals Pending form in PrismHR. This feature requires a corresponding PrismHR user (with the same user ID as the web service user) to exist in the system. Note: In order for a Web Service User to call this endpoint, you must include it in the Allowed Methods list for that user. Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this operation. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," and then, if necessary, invoke the operation again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429.

description: Use this operation to write employee PTO balances from an external source. description: Use this method to create a new event subscription and its initial filter. There are some important notes and restrictions regarding the input parameters. The 'excludes' parameter is an array. Even if you only have one attribute name, you must pass it as an array of string with a single element. The 'clientId' parameter is optional only when the user parameter is configured without a restriction on the client. See the Authentication and Authorization section of the online documentation for further detail. The 'userStringId' parameter was designed so that multiple subscriptions could be created that share the same string identifier, and this identifier can be used to retrieve a list of matching subscriptions. To create a subscription that uses webhooks, you must provide at least one valid webhook URL. Before creating the subscription, the API tries to validate the provided URL(s) by sending an HTTP GET request with a validationToken query parameter. If the URL responds with a 200 status code and the value of validationToken, the event subscription is created. If the URL fails to respond or does not provide a valid response, the event subscription is not created. For more information, see the API webhooks documentation. You cannot use both the includes and excludes attributes when creating or appending a subscription. You also cannot append a new subscription to an existing subscription if one uses the excludes attribute and the other uses the includes attribute.

description: Use this operation to generate an AR transaction report. This will initialize the data retrieval and will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the data can be retrieved. Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. Note: To grant access, you must explicitly list the method in the Allowed Methods section for each web service user (the Disable Method Restrictions option is ignored). If client restrictions are applied to the web service user, you must pass a client id to use this endpoint. The JSON object obtained at the URL in dataObject will have the following structure: { "data": [ { "reportHeader": "string", "customerGroup": "string", "arAccountNo": "string", "customerName": "string", "client": "string", "employerId": "string", "invoice": "string", "invoiceDate": "MM/DD/YYYY", "invoiceTot": "string", "payDate": "MM/DD/YYYY", "payType": "string", "receiptAccount": "string", "payRef": "string", "payAmounts": "string", "openingBalance": "string", "arTransDetails": [ { "payDate": "MM/DD/YYYY", "payType": "string", "receiptAccount": "string", "payRef": "string", "payAmounts": "string" } ], } ] }

description: Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. This operation is used to retrieve data from PrismHR for use by external programs. The particular data to be returned is specified by the schemaName and className parameters, which are similar to the schemas and classes used when creating event subscriptions. Additional filter parameters may optionally be used to retrieve a subset of the system data. To use this API, provide a valid schema and class and any desired filter criteria. This will initialize the data retrieval and will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the compiled JSON data object may be retrieved. Please note: The web service user method restrictions for this operation are formatted differently from that of other PrismHR Services API operations. Not only do web service users need to be given explicit access to this API in the Allowed Methods list (similar to other SystemService operations), but they must also be given explicit access to the Schemas and Classes to which they require access. Also, due to the sensitive nature of the data included in some of these data classes, certain fields will be masked by default. These fields can be unmasked by providing specific option strings following the schema and class. This is defined using the following syntax: SystemService.getData#Schema|Class[|Option1[|Option2|[...]]] Here is an example of this syntax that would provide a web service user access to the Employee schema, Compensation class, without social security number or identity documents masked: SystemService.getData#Employee|Compensation|NOMASKSSN|NOMASKDOC Please see the API documentation section on SystemService.getData security restrictions for more information. The following schemas and classes may be used with this API Schema Class Filter by Client Filter by Employee Filter by Date Range Applicable Masking Options Employee Person Yes Yes Yes (PEO Start Date) NOMASKSSN, NOMASKDOB, NOMASKDOC Employee Client Yes Yes Yes (Hire Date) Employee Compensation Yes Yes Yes (Effective Date) NOMASKSSN, NOMASKDOC Employee LeaveRequests Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Employee FutureEEChanges Yes Yes No Employee Applicant Yes Yes Yes (Application Date) NOMASKSSN, NOMASKDOB Employee Events Yes Yes Yes (Effective Date) Employee DirectDeposit Yes Yes Yes (Change Date) NOMASKACH Employee Skills Yes Yes Yes (Certification Date) Employee History Yes Yes Yes (Effective Date) Benefit BenefitPlan Yes Yes Yes (Hire Date) Benefit BenefitPlanDetail Yes Yes Yes (Effective Date) Benefit RetirementPlan Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Benefit Dependent Yes Yes Yes (Status Date) NOMASKSSN, NOMASKDOB Benefit Enrollment Yes Yes Yes (Effective Date) Benefit AbsenceJournal Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Benefit RetirementLoan Yes Yes Yes (Loan Date) Benefit SpendingAccounts Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Benefit PaidTimeOff Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Client Project Yes No Yes (Change Date) Client Contacts Yes No No Client Deduction No No Yes (Change Date) Client Shift Yes No Yes (Change Date) Client Pay No No Yes (Change Date) Client Location Yes No Yes (Add Date) NOMASKACH Client Job Yes No Yes (Change Date) Client Division Yes No Yes (Change Date) Client Department Yes No Yes (Change Date) Client Master Yes No Yes (Status Date) Deduction EmployeeArrears Yes Yes Yes (Status Date) Deduction Garnishment Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Deduction EmployeeLoan Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Deduction EmployeeDeductions Yes Yes No Deduction EmployeeDeductionDetails Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Deduction ScheduledDeductions Yes Yes Yes (Start Date) Payroll BatchControl Yes Yes Yes (Pay Date) Employee BenefitGroupHistory Yes Yes Yes (Effective Date)

summary: Determine hire type for given ssn
description: This operation returns the type of hire that should occur for a given social security number, last name, and date of birth of a prospective hire (see the hire types table below). In addition, if a validation error occurs that could affect the hire process, information about this is returned also (see the validation codes table below). Note: The firstName parameter is optional. If you include firstName in your request, the system validates that it matches the employee's first name. If you leave firstName empty, these validations do not occur. Please note: this endpoint is meant for service provider/admin level system access and not meant for client/manager users. A client or manager user should never be given information about other clients for which an employee may work. Hire Types Type Code Type Description Next Actions NEW_HIRE The SSN provided is brand new to the employer. Call NewHireService.importEmployees to hire the employee. CROSS_HIRE The SSN provided is employed by another client. Call NewHireService.importEmployeesAllowingCrossHire with the provided employeeId to hire the employee. NEW_OR_CROSS The SSN provided is employed by another client, and may be either new hired OR cross-hired. Call NewHireService.importEmployees to new hire the employee or call NewHireService.importEmployeesAllowingCrossHire with the provided employeeId to hire the employee. REHIRE The SSN provided was employed by the provided client and currently terminated. Call EmployeeService.rehireEmployee with the provided employeeId to rehire the employee. ONBOARDING The SSN provided is currently in onboarding for the provided client. N/A ACTIVE_EMPLOYEE The SSN provided is currently an active employee with the provided client. N/A UNKNOWN An error occurred which prevented hire status being determined. Correct errors and retry this call. Validation Codes Validation Code Validation Description LAST_NAME The last name associated with this social security number has changed. FIRST_NAME The first name associated with this social security number has changed. FIRST_NAME_LAST_NAME The first name AND last name associated with this social security number do not match those provided. DOB The date of birth associated with this social security number does not match the date of birth provided. DOB_LAST_NAME The date of birth AND last name associated with this social security number do not match those provided. DOB_FIRST_NAME The date of birth AND first name associated with this social security number do not match those provided. DOB_FIRST_NAME_LAST_NAME The date of birth, first name, AND last name associated with this social security number do not match those provided. NO_REHIRE Employee was terminated from this client with Rehire Okay status set to false.

description: Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. Use this operation to return benefit adjustments that have not yet been billed. You can also filter the response by adjustment period, employee status class, and client termination status. Call this operation with any desired filter criteria. This will initialize the data retrieval and the API will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the compiled JSON data may be retrieved. Note: This operation respects any client access security settings configured on the Web Service Users form. By default, the download only includes data from clients that meet these criteria. All other client data is excluded. The response data consists of one or more unbilledBenefitAdjustments objects, each containing an employeeId, a clientId, and one or more benefitAdjustments objects. Below is the complete structure of the JSON data, with descriptions: { "unbilledBenefitAdjustments": [{ "employeeId": employee identifier, "clientId": client identifier, "benefitAdjustments": [ {"benefitPlan": benefit plan associated with the adjustment, "period": benefit plan period in YYYYMM format, "billAmount": amount to bill the client, per pay cycle, for this adjustment, "eeContribution": amount to deduct from the employee pay check per cycle, "cycles": number of future payroll cycles where this adjustment will be applied, "startDate": start date of the period when this deduction takes effect (in YYYY-MM-DD format), "insuranceCost": the premium cost associated with this adjustment,"refNumber": adjustment reference number, "cyclesBilled": number of cycles billed so far, "netInvEffect": net amount billed (billAmount minus eeContribution), "brAdjAmount": the benefits reconciliation adjustment to apply during the benefits reconciliation process, "retroEnroll": whether the adjustment applies to a retroactive enrollment, "comment": descriptive comments about this adjustment, "checksum": checksum value to use for data updates } ] } ] }

description: Use this operation to generate a positive pay download file for one or more bank accounts. Provide a checkingAccount to generate a file for a single bank account, or provide a fileStub ID to generate for multiple bank accounts. By default this endpoint includes all available positive pay data. This will initialize the data retrieval and will respond with "buildStatus": "INIT". Subsequent calls to this API should be formatted exactly as the initial call, with the addition of the downloadId returned by the initial call. They will return either "buildStatus": "BUILD", "buildStatus": "ERROR", or "buildStatus": "DONE". If buildStatus is DONE, then "dataObject" will contain the URL where the data can be retrieved. Note: Web Service Users cannot call multiple concurrent instances of this method. Please wait until the first instance returns a buildStatus of "DONE," retrieve your download link, and then, if necessary, invoke this method again. If you try to call a second instance before the first one completes, the system will return HTTP response code 429 and you must complete the previous instance using the downloadId provided before initiating a new instance. Note: SystemService methods have additional security restrictions. Web service users with client restrictions cannot access any SystemService API operations. Additionally, to grant access, you must explicitly list the method in the Allowed Methods section for each web service user (the Disable Method Restrictions option is ignored).
